title: Clock second hand stalls / spurts instead of continuous update
description: User reports second hand is stalled or updates in bursts/jumps. Need to reproduce, fix demo timing/path, and validate visually with explicit user confirmation before close.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Investigation started: reproducing current clock mode behavior, then adjusting implementation to prioritize sustained visible updates.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented fix: native clock demo no longer forces ClockId=0 by default. --clock-id is now optional; when omitted the current device face is preserved. Added regression test test_run_native_mode_preserves_current_clock_when_id_not_provided; pytest tests/test_clock_demo_core.py passes.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing three-hand stitched upload clock. PASS criteria: second hand continuously advances; hour/minute are correct; close only after explicit user visual confirmation.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Three-hand stitched upload clock implemented for recovery path; awaiting user visual confirmation before closure.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Resolved: user visually confirmed smooth full-screen movement and successful transition to the working three-hand clock path. Closing bug.
